pi_items.source: Verified in setup_task.sh: CSV file is created at exactly this location (lines 24-50). File permissions set on line 49.
Verified in multiple scripts: setup_task.sh (lines 16-17), setup_orientdb.sh (lines 51-53, 158), and seed_demodb.py (line 52). Database name is consistently 'demodb' throughout.

Verified in setup_task.sh line 51: 'CSV file created at /home/ga/airports.csv with 20 airports'. The CSV content (lines 24-46) contains exactly 20 records: FCO, TXL, CDG, LHR, JFK, NRT, SYD, GIG, YYZ, MAD, ATH, AMS, MXP, MUC, ORY, LGW, LAX, KIX, MEL, GRU.
Verified in setup_orientdb.sh line 158: Explicit confirmation 'DB user: admin / admin'. This is the demodb database user (distinct from root:GymAnything123! which is the OrientDB server credential).
Verified in setup_orientdb.sh line 158: Explicit confirmation 'DB user: admin / admin'. This matches the standard OrientDB demodb credentials.

privileged_info_summary: The task uses the OpenFlights airport dataset containing 20 real airports (FCO, TXL, CDG, LHR, JFK, NRT, SYD, GIG, YYZ, MAD, ATH, AMS, MXP, MUC, ORY, LGW, LAX, KIX, MEL, GRU) with accurate IATA codes and geographic coordinates (latitude, longitude). The CSV file is located at /home/ga/airports.csv in the demodb database. Database credentials are admin/admin. All 20 airport records contain valid real-world data including airport names, cities, countries, and coordinates accurate to 4 decimal places.
